title: Air-fryer pork belly bites
subtitle: Crispy five-spice pork belly chunks in the air fryer.
excerpt: Thick cubes of pork belly seasoned with Chinese five spice, soy sauce and garlic, cooked in the air fryer until the fat renders and the exterior turns deeply golden and crackling. Serves four as a starter.
sourceNotes: Air-fryer pork belly bites. Pork belly has been cooked throughout Chinese, Korean and Southeast Asian cuisines for centuries, with crispy preparations documented in Chinese cooking manuals from the Song dynasty. This recipe applies those flavour principles to a modern air-fryer method. The air fryer replicates the rendered, crispy-fat result of traditional Chinese roast pork (siu yuk) without a specialist oven.

content.text: Score the skin of the pork belly before marinating. The scoring allows the fat to render more quickly and the marinade to reach the meat. Cut the pieces no larger than 3 cm; larger pieces will not render in time. The resting period after cooking is important: the juices redistribute and the fat firms slightly.

items.name: Pork belly
items.prepNote: cut into 3 cm cubes, skin scored

content.text: Mix {{chinese-five-spice}} of five spice, {{soy-sauce-dark}} of dark soy sauce, {{garlic}} of minced garlic, {{dark-brown-sugar}} of sugar, {{ginger-root}} of grated ginger, {{rapeseed-oil}} of oil and {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt. Toss with {{pork-belly}} of pork belly pieces and marinate for at least 1 hour, ideally overnight. Air-fry at 200°C for 20 to 25 minutes, turning halfway, until the exterior is deeply golden and the fat is rendered. Rest for 5 minutes before serving.

items.fix: Cut to 3 cm maximum. Very thick layers of fat need longer; cook in smaller pieces or add 5 minutes.
items.cause: Pieces too large or too much fat for the cooking time.

items.fix: Reduce the brown sugar to 1 tsp if your air fryer runs hot. Check at 15 minutes and reduce temperature if colouring too quickly.
Cook in a single layer with space between pieces. Crowded pieces steam rather than crisp.
items.symptom: Pale and steaming rather than crisping

content.text: Crispy pork belly is a staple of Chinese roast-meat traditions and Korean street food. This air-fryer version is a simplified weeknight version of the slow-roasted originals. Serve with steamed rice and spring onion, or as a starter with a dipping sauce of soy sauce and rice vinegar.
